*This document and its contents are the confidential and proprietary property of Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. This synopsis is prepared solely for Pre-Submission discussion with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and may not be disclosed, distributed, or reproduced without the prior written authorization of Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.*

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[1. Study Title and Protocol Identifiers]{.underline}**

**Proposed Study Title:** A Prospective, Single-Arm, Multicenter Evaluation of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System for Pharmacomechanical Thrombectomy in Adult Patients with Acute Iliofemoral Deep Vein Thrombosis

**Protocol Number:** CLR-VC3-001

**Sponsor:** Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc., 8200 Excelsior Blvd, Suite 410, Minnetonka, MN 55343

**Sponsor Contact:** Linda Fessenden, Vice President of Regulatory Affairs, Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.

**Clinical Research Organization:** Stonebridge Clinical Research Organization (proposed CRO responsible for study management, site monitoring, and data management)

**Proposed Study Start Date:** Q4 2025

**Estimated Enrollment Completion:** Q2 2026

**[2. Device Description Summary]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is a catheter-based platform designed to perform pharmacomechanical thrombectomy in the peripheral venous vasculature. The system is intended for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the iliofemoral venous segment and consists of three principal components:

**(1) VascuClear Catheter.** The VascuClear Catheter is a single-use, 10 French (Fr) diameter, 65 cm working length, over-the-wire catheter designed for percutaneous insertion into the venous system. The catheter incorporates a rotating helical impeller at its distal tip that macerates and fragments thrombus upon contact. The catheter also features a proprietary integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen that enables the simultaneous local delivery of tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) directly at the thrombectomy treatment site. This integrated design allows for combined pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- mechanical thrombus fragmentation and aspiration occurring concurrently with localized thrombolytic drug delivery --- within a single catheter platform. The catheter is provided sterile and is intended for single use only.

**(2) VascuClear Console.** The VascuClear Console is a reusable electromechanical drive unit that powers the catheter\'s helical impeller at operator-selected speeds up to 12,000 revolutions per minute (RPM). The console also houses an integrated aspiration pump capable of generating negative pressure up to −650 mmHg, providing continuous aspiration of macerated thrombus material through the catheter lumen during the thrombectomy procedure. The console features a touchscreen user interface for real-time monitoring and adjustment of impeller speed and aspiration pressure. The console is designed for use in interventional radiology suites, catheterization laboratories, and operating rooms.

**(3) Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen.** The proprietary thrombolytic infusion lumen is integrated into the VascuClear Catheter shaft and provides a dedicated channel for the infusion of thrombolytic agents at controlled rates during the thrombectomy procedure. The infusion lumen connects to a standard intravenous infusion pump at its proximal hub and delivers the thrombolytic agent through multiple side-holes positioned along the distal treatment zone of the catheter, enabling uniform drug distribution across the target thrombus segment. This combined delivery architecture is a core feature of the VascuClear 3000 system and differentiates it from conventional mechanical-only thrombectomy catheters.

**Regulatory Classification.** The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is classified under product code GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\") and regulated under 21 CFR § 870.1250. The identified predicate device for the planned 510(k) submission is the ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System (K192847), manufactured by Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc., which received 510(k) clearance on August 14, 2020.

**[3. Study Rationale and Background]{.underline}**

Acute deep vein thrombosis (DVT) of the iliofemoral venous segment is a clinically significant condition that poses substantial morbidity to affected patients. Proximal DVT involving the iliac and common femoral veins carries an elevated risk of pulmonary embolism (PE) and is the primary risk factor for the subsequent development of post-thrombotic syndrome (PTS), a chronic condition characterized by pain, swelling, skin changes, and venous ulceration. Conventional treatment with systemic anticoagulation alone, while effective in preventing thrombus propagation, does not actively remove existing thrombus and may leave patients with significant residual venous obstruction and valve damage that contributes to the later development of PTS.

Pharmacomechanical thrombectomy has emerged as a treatment modality that combines the active mechanical removal of thrombus with the simultaneous local delivery of thrombolytic agents to the affected venous segment. This combined approach is intended to achieve more complete thrombus clearance than either mechanical thrombectomy or catheter-directed thrombolysis alone, with the potential to reduce procedure time and total thrombolytic dose compared with stand-alone catheter-directed thrombolysis techniques.

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is designed to provide combined pharmacomechanical thrombectomy in a single integrated catheter system, simplifying the procedural workflow by eliminating the need for sequential device exchanges between mechanical thrombectomy and thrombolytic infusion catheters. The integrated helical impeller provides effective thrombus maceration and aspiration, while the dedicated thrombolytic infusion lumen enables concurrent localized delivery of tPA to the treatment site.

Clinical data generated in the proposed study is intended to support the 510(k) submission for the VascuClear 3000 by demonstrating the safety and effectiveness of the device in its proposed indication for use. Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. has previously received three 510(k) clearances from FDA for Class II vascular access accessories (K173291, K184560, and K201188), and the company has established quality systems and regulatory infrastructure to support Class II device development and commercialization. The ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System (K192847), manufactured by Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc., serves as the primary predicate device for the planned 510(k) submission, and the proposed clinical study is designed to generate supportive clinical evidence demonstrating that the VascuClear 3000 performs comparably in the intended patient population.

**[4. Study Objectives]{.underline}**

**[4.1 Primary Objective]{.underline}**

The primary objective of this study is to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System for percutaneous removal of thrombus and simultaneous local delivery of tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) in adult patients presenting with acute iliofemoral deep vein thrombosis (symptom onset ≤14 days prior to the index procedure).

**[4.2 Secondary Objective]{.underline}**

The secondary objective of this study is to assess iliofemoral vein patency at 30 days following treatment with the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System, as measured by duplex ultrasound assessment of the treated venous segment.

**[5. Study Design]{.underline}**

This study is designed as a single-arm, prospective, multicenter clinical evaluation of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System. The study does not include a randomized control arm or comparator group. All enrolled patients will receive treatment with the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System with concurrent tPA infusion through the device\'s integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen, in accordance with the procedure described in Section 8.2 of this synopsis.

**Number of Sites.** The study will be conducted at five (5) clinical sites across the United States. Participating sites will be selected from academic medical centers and community hospitals with established interventional radiology or vascular surgery programs and demonstrated experience in catheter-directed venous interventions. Site selection criteria will include sufficient patient volume, appropriate catheterization laboratory or interventional suite facilities, and clinical investigator experience with pharmacomechanical thrombectomy techniques.

**Enrollment Target.** A total of sixty (60) patients will be enrolled in the study. The enrollment target has been established based on the statistical considerations described in Section 11 and is intended to provide adequate clinical experience with the device across multiple operators and clinical settings.

**Study Characterization.** This study is designed as a post-clearance supportive evidence collection effort to document clinical outcomes with the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System in the intended patient population and indication for use.

**[6. Regulatory Status and IDE Determination]{.underline}**

An Investigational Device Exemption (IDE) is not anticipated for this study. The clinical study is designed as a post-clearance data collection effort, and the VascuClear 3000 is expected to have received 510(k) clearance prior to study initiation. Accordingly, the study will be conducted under the cleared device labeling and no IDE application will be submitted to FDA.

The clinical data generated in this study will serve as supportive clinical evidence and may be included in the 510(k) submission package or maintained as a post-market clinical study to supplement the device\'s clinical evidence base. Because the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System will be used in accordance with its cleared indications for use and within the parameters described in the approved device labeling, the study is not considered a significant risk device study. The device will be used by trained interventional physicians in an appropriate clinical setting, and the procedures performed in this study are consistent with standard-of-care pharmacomechanical thrombectomy techniques currently employed in clinical practice.

Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. will maintain a study master file and will ensure that all clinical data are collected, managed, and reported in accordance with applicable regulatory requirements and good clinical practice standards. The Sponsor will notify FDA of the study results as appropriate and will include relevant clinical data in future regulatory submissions as needed.

**[7. Study Population]{.underline}**

**[7.1 Target Population]{.underline}**

The target population for this study comprises adult patients presenting with acute proximal deep vein thrombosis involving the iliofemoral venous segment (common femoral vein, external iliac vein, and/or common iliac vein), confirmed by diagnostic imaging. Eligible patients must have symptomatic DVT with onset of symptoms within 14 days of the planned thrombectomy procedure.

**[7.2 Inclusion Criteria]{.underline}**

Patients must meet all of the following inclusion criteria to be eligible for enrollment in this study:

> 1\. Age 18 to 75 years, inclusive, at the time of informed consent.
>
> 2\. Acute iliofemoral deep vein thrombosis confirmed by duplex ultrasound imaging, with involvement of the common femoral vein and/or iliac vein(s).
>
> 3\. Symptom onset ≤14 days prior to the planned index procedure, based on patient history and clinical assessment.
>
> 4\. Ability to provide written informed consent and willingness to participate in the study.
>
> 5\. Willingness and ability to comply with the study follow-up schedule, including the 30-day follow-up visit.
>
> 6\. Adequate femoral or popliteal venous access for catheter insertion, as determined by the treating physician based on physical examination and/or imaging assessment.

**[7.3 Exclusion Criteria]{.underline}**

Patients meeting any of the following exclusion criteria will be excluded from study participation:

> 1\. Active bleeding or clinically significant hemorrhage at the time of screening or at any time prior to the index procedure.
>
> 2\. Major surgery within 30 days prior to the planned index procedure.
>
> 3\. Known history of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT).
>
> 4\. Pregnancy or suspected pregnancy, confirmed by serum or urine pregnancy testing in women of childbearing potential.
>
> 5\. Life expectancy of less than 6 months due to comorbid conditions, as determined by the treating physician.
>
> 6\. Known allergy or hypersensitivity to catheter component materials, including polyurethane and silicone.
>
> 7\. Target vessel diameter of less than 6 mm as measured by duplex ultrasound or other cross-sectional imaging, which may preclude safe passage of the 10 Fr VascuClear Catheter.

Patients who meet all inclusion criteria and none of the exclusion criteria will be considered eligible for enrollment. Final eligibility determination will be made by the site Principal Investigator.

**[8. Study Procedures]{.underline}**

**[8.1 Pre-Procedure Assessments]{.underline}**

The following assessments will be completed prior to the index thrombectomy procedure:

> • **Baseline Duplex Ultrasound.** A duplex ultrasound examination of the affected lower extremity will be performed to confirm the location, extent, and chronicity of the iliofemoral DVT and to assess target vessel diameter.
>
> • **Medical History and Physical Examination.** A complete medical history and focused physical examination will be performed, including assessment of the affected limb for signs and symptoms of DVT (pain, swelling, erythema, warmth).
>
> • **Laboratory Assessments.** Baseline laboratory studies will include complete blood count (CBC), coagulation panel (prothrombin time/international normalized ratio \[PT/INR\] and activated partial thromboplastin time \[aPTT\]), and serum creatinine.
>
> • **Eligibility Review.** The site Principal Investigator will review all inclusion and exclusion criteria and confirm patient eligibility prior to proceeding with the index procedure.
>
> • **Informed Consent.** Written informed consent will be obtained from all patients prior to any study-related procedures, per institutional requirements.

**[8.2 Index Procedure --- Thrombectomy with tPA Infusion]{.underline}**

The index thrombectomy procedure will be performed in a catheterization laboratory, interventional radiology suite, or operating room equipped with fluoroscopic imaging capability. The procedure will be performed under conscious sedation or general anesthesia, per institutional protocol and at the discretion of the treating physician. The procedural steps are as follows:

> 1\. **Vascular Access.** Percutaneous venous access will be obtained under ultrasound guidance via the femoral vein (common femoral or popliteal approach), as determined by the treating physician based on the anatomic location of the thrombus and venous access considerations.
>
> 2\. **Guidewire Advancement.** A guidewire will be advanced across the thrombosed iliofemoral venous segment under fluoroscopic guidance. An initial diagnostic venogram may be performed to delineate the extent of the thrombus and to establish baseline thrombus burden for subsequent comparison.
>
> 3\. **Catheter Delivery.** The VascuClear 3000 Catheter (10 Fr, 65 cm working length) will be advanced over the guidewire to the treatment site within the thrombosed venous segment. Catheter positioning will be confirmed by fluoroscopy.
>
> 4\. **Console Activation.** The VascuClear Console will be activated, and the helical impeller will be engaged at a physician-selected rotational speed up to a maximum of 12,000 RPM. Simultaneously, the integrated aspiration pump will be activated, generating negative aspiration pressure up to −650 mmHg. These settings may be adjusted at the discretion of the treating physician based on the observed procedural response and thrombus characteristics.
>
> 5\. **Thrombolytic Infusion.** Concurrent with mechanical thrombectomy, tissue plasminogen activator (alteplase) infusion will be initiated through the integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen at a rate of 0.5 to 1.0 mg/hr. The tPA concentration and preparation will be performed per institutional pharmacy protocol. Physician-specified thrombolytic agents may be used per institutional practice at the treating physician\'s discretion.
>
> 6\. **Catheter Passes.** The VascuClear 3000 Catheter will be passed through the thrombosed venous segment to achieve thrombus maceration, fragmentation, and aspiration. Multiple passes through the treatment zone are permitted at the discretion of the treating physician.
>
> 7\. **Completion Venography.** Following the mechanical thrombectomy procedure, a completion venogram will be performed to assess residual thrombus burden in the treated segment. The degree of thrombus removal will be assessed visually and documented.
>
> 8\. **Extended Infusion (If Applicable).** If residual thrombus burden exceeds 50% on completion venography, the VascuClear Catheter may be left in situ for extended thrombolytic infusion for up to 72 hours, per the device Instructions for Use (IFU). During extended infusion, the helical impeller will not be activated, and the catheter will function as a thrombolytic infusion catheter only. Patients with the catheter left in situ will be monitored in an intensive care unit or step-down unit per institutional protocol.
>
> 9\. **Catheter Removal.** The VascuClear Catheter will be removed when the treating physician determines that adequate thrombus clearance has been achieved, or when the maximum in-situ catheter dwell time (72 hours) has been reached, whichever occurs first. A final venogram may be performed at the time of catheter removal to document the final treatment result.

Hemostasis at the venous access site will be achieved by manual compression or other standard techniques per institutional practice.

**[8.3 Post-Procedure Care]{.underline}**

Following completion of the thrombectomy procedure and catheter removal, patients will be monitored in the hospital setting for a minimum of 24 hours. During the post-procedure monitoring period, the venous access site will be evaluated for bleeding, hematoma formation, and other local complications. Vital signs, including heart rate, blood pressure, respiratory rate, and oxygen saturation, will be monitored at regular intervals per institutional nursing protocols.

Anticoagulation therapy will be initiated following the procedure per institutional protocol. A typical regimen consists of intravenous unfractionated heparin bridged to an oral anticoagulant (warfarin or a direct oral anticoagulant), with the specific agent, dosing, and duration determined by the treating physician in accordance with institutional standards of care and applicable clinical practice guidelines.

**[9. Study Endpoints]{.underline}**

**[9.1 Primary Endpoint]{.underline}**

The primary endpoint of this study is the percentage of patients achieving ≥50% thrombus removal at 24 hours post-procedure, as assessed by contrast venography of the treated iliofemoral venous segment. Thrombus removal will be quantified using a standardized semi-quantitative venographic scoring system based on the modified Marder scoring methodology (or an equivalent validated scoring system). Each treated venous segment will be scored at baseline (pre-procedure venogram) and at the 24-hour post-procedure venographic assessment. Treatment success is defined as a ≥50% reduction in venographic thrombus burden score compared to the baseline assessment.

The performance goal for this study is that at least 70% of treated patients will achieve the primary endpoint (i.e., ≥70% of the 60 enrolled patients must demonstrate ≥50% thrombus removal at 24-hour venography).

**[9.2 Secondary Endpoints]{.underline}**

The secondary endpoints of this study are as follows:

> • **Iliofemoral Vein Patency at 30 Days.** Patency of the treated iliofemoral venous segment at 30 days post-procedure, assessed by duplex ultrasound. Patency is defined as restoration of antegrade flow in the treated segment with ≤50% residual stenosis.
>
> • **Technical Success Rate.** The proportion of cases in which the VascuClear 3000 Catheter was successfully delivered to the target treatment site and the thrombectomy system (impeller and aspiration) was successfully activated.
>
> • **Procedural Duration.** The time from initial vascular access to final catheter removal, measured in minutes (excluding any extended in-situ infusion period).
>
> • **Total tPA Dose Administered.** The cumulative dose of tissue plasminogen activator (alteplase) administered through the device\'s thrombolytic infusion lumen, reported in milligrams (mg).
>
> • **Length of Hospital Stay.** Duration of hospitalization from the date of the index procedure to hospital discharge, reported in days.

**[9.3 Safety Endpoints]{.underline}**

The safety of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System will be evaluated through the incidence of major adverse events (MAEs) occurring through 30 days post-procedure. Major adverse events are defined as follows:

> • Death from any cause
>
> • Symptomatic pulmonary embolism
>
> • Major bleeding, defined as bleeding requiring blood transfusion (≥2 units packed red blood cells) or requiring surgical or interventional re-intervention for hemostasis
>
> • Vessel perforation
>
> • Device malfunction requiring surgical or interventional intervention

All major adverse events will be documented and tabulated for the study population.

**[10. Follow-Up Schedule]{.underline}**

The following table summarizes the study visit schedule and the assessments to be performed at each timepoint:

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Visit**                **Timepoint**           **Assessments**
  ------------------------ ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Baseline / Screening     Pre-Procedure           Duplex ultrasound, laboratory assessments (CBC, coagulation panel, serum creatinine), medical history, physical examination, informed consent, eligibility review

  Index Procedure          Day 0                   Thrombectomy with concurrent tPA infusion per protocol (Section 8.2)

  24-Hour Post-Procedure   Day 1                   Contrast venography (primary endpoint assessment), clinical assessment, post-procedure laboratory assessments

  30-Day Follow-Up         Day 30 (± 7 days)       Duplex ultrasound (secondary endpoint --- vein patency assessment), clinical assessment, adverse event review
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The 30-day follow-up visit is the final study visit. Patients will be discharged from the study following completion of the 30-day assessment. No additional follow-up visits are planned beyond the 30-day timepoint. Patients requiring ongoing clinical care for their underlying venous thromboembolism will continue to receive standard-of-care treatment from their managing physicians outside of the study protocol.

**[11. Statistical Considerations]{.underline}**

**[11.1 Sample Size Justification]{.underline}**

A total of sixty (60) patients will be enrolled in this study. The sample size was selected to provide adequate clinical experience with the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System across multiple operators and clinical sites, and to support a statistically rigorous evaluation of the primary endpoint.

The sample size calculation is based on the following assumptions: assuming a true thrombus removal success rate of 80% (i.e., the expected proportion of patients achieving ≥50% thrombus removal at 24 hours), a sample of 60 patients provides greater than 95% confidence that the lower bound of the exact binomial 95% confidence interval for the observed success rate will exceed the pre-specified performance goal of 70%. The calculation was performed using exact binomial methods and accounts for the single-arm study design with a pre-specified objective performance criterion.

**[11.2 Analysis Populations]{.underline}**

Two analysis populations are defined for this study:

> • **Intent-to-Treat (ITT) Population.** All patients who are enrolled in the study and undergo the index thrombectomy procedure with the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System. The ITT population is the primary analysis population for all endpoints.
>
> • **Per-Protocol (PP) Population.** All patients in the ITT population who complete the 24-hour post-procedure venographic assessment without major protocol deviations. The PP population will be used for supportive analyses of the primary endpoint.

**[11.3 Primary Analysis]{.underline}**

The primary analysis will consist of the proportion of patients in the ITT population achieving ≥50% thrombus removal at 24-hour venography, reported with an exact 95% binomial confidence interval. The study will be considered to have met its primary performance goal if the lower bound of the 95% confidence interval exceeds 70%.

**[11.4 Secondary and Safety Analyses]{.underline}**

All secondary endpoints will be summarized using descriptive statistics (means, standard deviations, medians, and ranges for continuous variables; frequencies and percentages for categorical variables). Safety analysis will consist of tabulation of all major adverse events by category, severity, and relatedness to the study device or procedure.

**[11.5 Missing Data]{.underline}**

Patients who are enrolled but do not complete the 24-hour post-procedure venographic assessment for any reason will be classified as treatment failures in the ITT analysis. No imputation of missing data will be performed.

**[12. Data Safety Monitoring]{.underline}**

Safety monitoring for this study will be conducted by the study Sponsor, Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc., and Stonebridge Clinical Research Organization on an ongoing basis throughout the enrollment and follow-up phases of the study. The Principal Investigator at each participating clinical site will be responsible for the assessment and reporting of adverse events occurring in enrolled patients at that site.

Aggregate safety data will be reviewed by the Sponsor\'s clinical team on a periodic basis during the conduct of the study. If a safety signal is identified during the course of the study, the Sponsor will evaluate the nature and severity of the signal and determine whether study modifications, including protocol amendments, enrollment suspension, or study termination, are warranted.

**[13. Informed Consent]{.underline}**

Written informed consent will be obtained from all study participants prior to any study-related procedures, in accordance with institutional requirements and applicable federal regulations governing the protection of human subjects (21 CFR Part 50). A model informed consent form will be developed by the Sponsor in collaboration with Stonebridge Clinical Research Organization and will be submitted for Institutional Review Board (IRB) approval at each participating clinical site. The informed consent form is currently in development and will be finalized following receipt of FDA feedback from the Pre-Submission meeting.

**[14. Institutional Review Board (IRB) Oversight]{.underline}**

The study protocol, informed consent form, and all study-related materials will be submitted for review and approval by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Ethics Committee at each participating clinical site prior to the initiation of any study-related activities at that site. No patient enrollment or study procedures will be conducted at any site until written IRB approval has been obtained. Continuing review of the study will be conducted per each IRB\'s established requirements and applicable institutional policies.

**[15. Study Timeline]{.underline}**

The anticipated study timeline is as follows:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Milestone**                                 **Estimated Date**
  --------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Protocol Finalization                         Q3 2025

  Site Selection and IRB Submissions            Q3 -- Q4 2025

  First Patient Enrollment                      Q4 2025

  Enrollment Completion (60 patients)           Q2 2026

  Last Patient, Last Visit (30-Day Follow-Up)   Q3 2026

  Clinical Study Report Finalization            Q4 2026
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The overall study duration from first patient enrollment to clinical study report finalization is estimated to be approximately 12 months. Enrollment pace is anticipated at approximately 2 to 3 patients per site per month, which is considered achievable based on the expected patient volumes at the selected clinical sites. Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. will monitor enrollment rates on an ongoing basis and may add additional clinical sites if enrollment is slower than anticipated.

**[16. Study Budget Summary]{.underline}**

The estimated total cost of the proposed clinical study is approximately **\$2.8 million**. The study will be funded entirely by Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. The principal budget categories include clinical site costs (investigator fees, institutional overhead, imaging and laboratory costs), CRO management and monitoring fees (Stonebridge Clinical Research Organization), core laboratory fees for independent venographic and ultrasound assessments, clinical data management and biostatistical analysis, regulatory support and submissions, and Sponsor overhead. A detailed study budget will be finalized during the site contracting process and will be available upon request.

**[17. References]{.underline}**

1\. Enden T, Haig Y, Klow NE, et al. \"Long-term outcome after additional catheter-directed thrombolysis versus standard treatment for acute iliofemoral deep vein thrombosis (the CaVenT study): a randomised controlled trial.\" *Lancet.* 2012;379(9810):31--38.

2\. Vedantham S, Goldhaber SZ, Julian JA, et al. \"Pharmacomechanical catheter-directed thrombolysis for deep-vein thrombosis.\" *N Engl J Med.* 2017;377(23):2240--2252.

3\. Comerota AJ, Kearon C, Gu CS, et al. \"Endovascular thrombus removal for acute iliofemoral deep vein thrombosis.\" *Circulation.* 2019;139(9):1162--1173.

4\. U.S. Food and Drug Administration. 510(k) Summary --- ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System (K192847). August 14, 2020.

5\. Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System Bench Testing Data. Document No. CLR-VC3-BENCH-001. 2025.

6\. International Organization for Standardization. ISO 14155:2020 --- Clinical Investigation of Medical Devices for Human Subjects --- Good Clinical Practice.

7\. U.S. Code of Federal Regulations, Title 21, Part 50 --- Protection of Human Subjects.

**[1. Device Description]{.underline}**

The VascuClear™ 3000 Thrombectomy System is manufactured by Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. The VascuClear 3000 is a single-use, over-the-wire catheter-based mechanical thrombectomy system designed for the percutaneous removal of thrombus from the iliofemoral venous segment in patients with acute proximal deep vein thrombosis (DVT) with symptom onset of fourteen (14) days or less. The VascuClear 3000 is designed for combined pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- simultaneous mechanical thrombus removal and localized drug delivery.

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System comprises three principal components:

**1. VascuClear Catheter.** The VascuClear Catheter is a single-use, sterile, 10 French (Fr) diameter catheter with a 65 cm working length. The catheter incorporates a rotating helical impeller at the distal tip designed for mechanical maceration of thrombus within the target vessel. The catheter features an integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen that allows for simultaneous local delivery of thrombolytic agents --- such as tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) or other physician-specified thrombolytic agents --- directly at the thrombectomy site during mechanical thrombus removal. The catheter is compatible with a standard 0.035-inch guidewire and requires an introducer sheath of 11 Fr or greater. The catheter is supplied sterile in a peel-pouch package and is sterilized by ethylene oxide (EtO). The catheter is intended for single use only and must not be resterilized or reprocessed.

**2. VascuClear Console.** The VascuClear Console is a reusable electromechanical drive unit that powers the catheter\'s helical impeller at variable speeds up to a maximum of 12,000 revolutions per minute (RPM). The console includes an integrated aspiration pump capable of generating negative pressure up to −650 mmHg for thrombus aspiration. Console firmware controls impeller speed, aspiration pressure, and system status, and provides real-time operational feedback to the operator via an integrated LCD display. The display presents impeller speed, aspiration pressure, system alerts, and procedural status indicators. The console is designed for repeated clinical use and is intended to be cleaned and maintained per the manufacturer\'s instructions following each procedure.

**3. Proprietary Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen.** The thrombolytic infusion lumen is integrated into the VascuClear Catheter and allows simultaneous local delivery of tPA or other physician-specified thrombolytic agents at the thrombectomy site during mechanical thrombus removal. This feature enables pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- the concurrent application of mechanical thrombus maceration and aspiration with localized thrombolytic drug infusion --- within a single catheter platform.

The VascuClear Catheter may remain in situ for up to 72 hours for extended infusion therapy in cases where prolonged thrombolytic delivery is clinically indicated. When the treating physician determines that the initial thrombectomy session has not achieved satisfactory thrombus removal, the catheter may be left in position within the target vessel segment to allow continued thrombolytic infusion over an extended period, followed by a repeat thrombectomy session or catheter removal.

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is intended to provide an integrated approach to the management of acute iliofemoral DVT by combining mechanical thrombus removal and local thrombolytic drug delivery within a single, catheter-based platform.

**[2. Indications for Use]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is indicated for the percutaneous removal of thrombus and simultaneous local delivery of physician-specified thrombolytic agents in adult patients with acute deep vein thrombosis (symptom onset ≤14 days) of the iliofemoral venous segment.

The intended patient population is adult patients eighteen (18) years of age and older who present with acute proximal DVT involving the iliofemoral venous segment and who are candidates for catheter-directed thrombectomy. The indication is specific to the iliofemoral venous segment, which includes the common iliac vein, the external iliac vein, and the common femoral vein.

The device is intended for use by qualified physicians trained in interventional vascular procedures in a catheterization laboratory or equivalent hospital setting equipped with appropriate fluoroscopic imaging, hemodynamic monitoring, and resuscitation capabilities. The VascuClear 3000 is not intended for use in the arterial vasculature, the cerebral vasculature, or the pulmonary vasculature.

**[3. Contraindications]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is contraindicated in the following conditions:

1\. Active internal bleeding or a known condition predisposing the patient to active hemorrhage at a site other than the vascular access site.

2\. Known allergy or hypersensitivity to catheter component materials, including but not limited to polyurethane, stainless steel, and barium sulfate.

3\. Target vessel diameter less than 6 mm, as determined by pre-procedural imaging, due to increased risk of vessel injury from the 10 Fr catheter and rotating impeller.

Physicians should exercise clinical judgment in evaluating additional patient-specific factors that may influence the risk-benefit profile of catheter-based pharmacomechanical thrombectomy.

**[4. Warnings]{.underline}**

Read all instructions carefully before use. Failure to follow these instructions may result in patient injury.

1\. The VascuClear Catheter is for single use only. Do not resterilize or reuse. Reuse of the catheter may result in device malfunction, cross-contamination, or patient injury. The structural integrity, sterility, and functional performance of the catheter cannot be assured after a single use.

2\. Do not exceed the maximum impeller speed of 12,000 RPM. Operation of the impeller at speeds exceeding the rated maximum may cause mechanical failure of the impeller or catheter shaft and may result in vessel damage, perforation, or dissection.

3\. Do not exceed aspiration pressure of −650 mmHg. Application of excessive negative pressure may result in vessel collapse, hemolysis, significant blood loss, or damage to the venous endothelium.

4\. Use of the device in vessels smaller than 6 mm in diameter may increase the risk of vessel perforation, dissection, or intimal injury. Pre-procedural imaging should be used to confirm that the target vessel diameter is adequate for the 10 Fr catheter and rotating impeller.

5\. The thrombolytic infusion lumen should be used only with physician-specified thrombolytic agents. The physician is responsible for selecting the appropriate thrombolytic agent and dosing regimen based on clinical judgment, institutional protocols, and the drug manufacturer\'s prescribing information. Clearfield Medical Devices does not provide recommendations regarding thrombolytic agent selection, dosing, or duration of therapy.

6\. Monitor patients for signs of bleeding during and after thrombolytic infusion. Thrombolytic agents carry inherent risks of hemorrhagic complications, including but not limited to access site bleeding, retroperitoneal hemorrhage, gastrointestinal hemorrhage, and intracranial hemorrhage. Appropriate hemodynamic monitoring and laboratory surveillance should be maintained throughout the period of thrombolytic infusion.

7\. The device should be used only under fluoroscopic guidance. Do not advance, reposition, or operate the catheter without continuous fluoroscopic visualization. Catheter manipulation without fluoroscopic guidance may result in vessel injury, catheter malposition, or unintended deployment of the impeller outside the target treatment zone.

8\. Ensure the guidewire is properly positioned before advancing the catheter. Advancing the VascuClear Catheter without adequate guidewire support may result in vessel injury, catheter kinking, or inability to reach the target thrombus. Guidewire position should be confirmed fluoroscopically prior to and during catheter advancement.

9\. Air may enter the system if connections are not properly secured. Ensure all Luer connections, aspiration tubing connections, and infusion line connections are tight, secure, and free of air before initiating aspiration or infusion. Air entrainment during aspiration may introduce air into the venous system and may result in air embolism.

10\. Do not use the device in patients with known or suspected venous malformations at the treatment site. Venous malformations, including aneurysmal dilation, venous webs, or structural abnormalities, may increase the risk of vessel injury or impeller entrapment during the thrombectomy procedure.

**[5. Precautions]{.underline}**

The following precautions should be observed when using the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System:

1\. The VascuClear 3000 should be used only by physicians who are trained and experienced in percutaneous interventional vascular procedures, including catheter-based thrombectomy techniques. Familiarity with venous anatomy, fluoroscopic imaging, venography, and the management of procedural complications is essential.

2\. Operator training on the VascuClear 3000 system is recommended prior to first clinical use. Training materials and in-service programs are available from Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. Physicians and catheterization laboratory staff who are unfamiliar with the system should complete the available training program before performing clinical procedures.

3\. Prior to use, inspect the catheter and packaging for damage. Examine the peel-pouch package for punctures, tears, or evidence of compromise to the sterile barrier. Do not use if the sterile barrier has been compromised or if any damage to the catheter is observed upon inspection.

4\. Verify compatibility of all system components before use. Use only VascuClear-branded catheters with the VascuClear Console. The use of non-compatible catheters or third-party components may result in device malfunction and is not supported by Clearfield Medical Devices.

5\. Patients should be monitored hemodynamically during and after the procedure. Continuous monitoring of vital signs, including blood pressure, heart rate, and oxygen saturation, is recommended throughout the thrombectomy procedure and during any period of extended thrombolytic infusion.

6\. Appropriate anticoagulation should be administered per institutional protocol. Systemic anticoagulation with heparin or other anticoagulant agents is typically employed during catheter-based venous interventions to reduce the risk of catheter-associated thrombosis. The treating physician should determine the appropriate anticoagulation regimen.

7\. If resistance is encountered during catheter advancement, do not force the catheter. Withdraw the catheter and reposition under fluoroscopic guidance. Forced advancement may result in vessel perforation, catheter damage, or catheter fracture.

8\. The physician is responsible for determining the appropriateness of thrombolytic agent selection, dosing, and duration of infusion based on patient-specific factors. Clearfield Medical Devices does not provide specific recommendations regarding drug selection or dosing parameters.

9\. Dispose of used catheters, aspiration tubing sets, infusion line connectors, and collection canisters in accordance with institutional biohazard waste protocols and applicable local, state, and federal regulations governing the disposal of medical waste.

**[6. MRI Safety Information]{.underline}**

**[VascuClear Catheter --- MR Conditional]{.underline}**

The VascuClear Catheter has been determined to be MR Conditional. Non-clinical testing has demonstrated that the VascuClear Catheter may be safely scanned under the following conditions:

> • Static magnetic field of 1.5 T and 3.0 T only
>
> • Maximum spatial gradient magnetic field of 3,000 Gauss/cm or less
>
> • Maximum whole-body-averaged specific absorption rate (SAR) of 2 W/kg for 15 minutes of continuous scanning

In non-clinical testing, the VascuClear Catheter produced a temperature rise of less than \[TBD\] °C at a maximum whole-body-averaged SAR of 2 W/kg for 15 minutes of continuous scanning in a \[TBD\] T MRI system.

MR image quality may be compromised if the area of interest is in the same area or relatively close to the position of the device. Image artifact may extend several centimeters from the metallic components of the catheter, depending on the pulse sequence type, imaging parameters, and magnetic field strength.

Refer to Clearfield Medical Devices technical documentation for complete MRI safety test data, including specific testing conditions, artifact characterization, and any additional use limitations.

**[VascuClear Console --- MR Unsafe]{.underline}**

The VascuClear Console is **MR Unsafe**. Do not bring the VascuClear Console into the MRI scanner room or the MRI-controlled access area. The console contains ferromagnetic components and electronic circuitry that may present a projectile hazard, may be damaged by the MRI environment, or may interfere with MRI system operation.

**[7. Device Components and Accessories]{.underline}**

  -------------------------------------- ------------------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  VascuClear Console                     VC3000-D           Reusable electromechanical drive unit with integrated aspiration pump   Powers impeller, controls aspiration, provides real-time LCD feedback

**[Required Components Not Supplied]{.underline}**

The following items are required for use of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System but are not included and must be sourced separately by the treating facility:

> • 0.035-inch guidewire (standard or stiff), as determined by physician preference
>
> • Introducer sheath, 11 Fr or greater
>
> • Standard contrast injection equipment for venography
>
> • Fluoroscopy system with adequate imaging capability for venous interventions
>
> • Thrombolytic agent (tPA or other physician-specified thrombolytic agent) --- not supplied; to be sourced by the treating facility per institutional formulary
>
> • Heparinized saline flush solution
>
> • Standard interventional procedure supplies (sterile drapes, syringes, stopcocks, etc.)

**[8. Instructions for Use --- Procedural Steps]{.underline}**

**[8.1 Pre-Procedure Preparation]{.underline}**

1\. **Console Setup.** Position the VascuClear Console on a stable surface adjacent to the procedure table. Connect the hospital-grade power cord to the console and to a grounded electrical outlet. Power on the console and allow the system to perform its automated self-check sequence. The LCD display will indicate system status; verify that the self-check completes without error messages. Confirm the firmware version displayed on the console matches the current approved firmware revision listed in the console operator manual.

2\. **Catheter Inspection.** Remove the VascuClear Catheter package from storage. Inspect the peel-pouch package for any evidence of damage, puncture, or moisture penetration. Verify that the sterile barrier is intact. Confirm the expiration date printed on the package label has not been exceeded. Do not use the catheter if the package is damaged or the expiration date has passed.

3\. **Catheter Preparation.** Open the catheter package using aseptic technique in accordance with institutional standards for sterile catheterization procedures. Using a syringe filled with heparinized saline, flush all catheter lumens --- including the guidewire lumen, aspiration lumen, and thrombolytic infusion lumen --- to ensure patency and to remove any residual air from the system. Inspect the catheter shaft and distal tip for any visible defects.

4\. **Guidewire and Sheath Compatibility.** Confirm that the guidewire to be used is a standard 0.035-inch guidewire and that the introducer sheath is 11 Fr or greater. Verify that the guidewire passes freely through the catheter guidewire lumen.

5\. **Thrombolytic Agent Preparation.** Prepare tPA or other thrombolytic agent as directed by the prescribing physician. Clearfield Medical Devices does not specify the thrombolytic agent, concentration, or preparation method. The physician is solely responsible for drug selection, reconstitution, dilution, and dosing. The thrombolytic agent should be prepared in accordance with the drug manufacturer\'s prescribing information and institutional pharmacy protocols.

**[8.2 Vascular Access and Catheter Insertion]{.underline}**

1\. Obtain percutaneous venous access using standard Seldinger technique. The access approach --- popliteal, femoral, or other venous access site --- is determined by the treating physician based on the location and extent of the iliofemoral thrombus and clinical considerations. Ultrasound-guided venous access is recommended.

2\. Place an introducer sheath of 11 Fr or greater into the access vein. Confirm sheath position with fluoroscopy and, if indicated, contrast venography.

3\. Advance a 0.035-inch guidewire through the introducer sheath and across the thrombosed iliofemoral venous segment under continuous fluoroscopic guidance. Confirm guidewire position within the target vessel segment. If the guidewire cannot be advanced across the thrombosed segment, the VascuClear Catheter should not be used.

4\. Advance the VascuClear Catheter over the positioned guidewire and through the introducer sheath into the target iliofemoral venous segment. Advance the catheter under continuous fluoroscopic visualization until the distal tip of the catheter, including the helical impeller, is positioned within the thrombosed segment.

5\. Confirm catheter position with contrast venography. Ensure the catheter is positioned within the target thrombus and that the impeller is within the thrombosed segment before proceeding.

**[8.3 Thrombectomy Procedure]{.underline}**

1\. **Connect System Components.** Attach the drive cable connector from the VascuClear Console to the proximal hub of the VascuClear Catheter. Ensure the connection is secure. Connect the aspiration tubing set from the catheter\'s aspiration port to the console\'s aspiration pump inlet. Attach the collection canister to the aspiration outlet. Connect the thrombolytic infusion line from the catheter\'s infusion port to the prepared thrombolytic agent infusion source.

2\. **Initiate Impeller Rotation.** Using the console controls, initiate impeller rotation at a low starting speed. A starting speed of approximately 3,000 RPM is recommended. Gradually increase impeller speed to the therapeutic speed as clinically indicated, based on the characteristics of the thrombus and the operator\'s assessment of treatment response. Do not exceed the maximum impeller speed of 12,000 RPM.

3\. **Activate Aspiration.** Activate the console\'s aspiration pump. Set the aspiration negative pressure to the target level as clinically indicated. Do not exceed the maximum aspiration pressure of −650 mmHg. Monitor the aspiration tubing and collection canister for evidence of thrombus aspiration.

4\. **Begin Thrombolytic Infusion.** Initiate thrombolytic infusion through the integrated infusion lumen. Infuse tPA or other physician-specified thrombolytic agent at a rate determined by the treating physician. A typical infusion flow rate range is 2--10 mL/min, but the actual rate should be determined by clinical judgment and institutional protocol.

5\. **Perform Thrombectomy.** Under continuous fluoroscopic guidance, advance and retract the catheter through the thrombosed segment to macerate and aspirate the thrombus. The rotating helical impeller macerates the thrombus while the aspiration pump removes fragmented thrombus material. Simultaneous thrombolytic infusion provides localized pharmacological thrombolysis at the treatment site.

6\. **Monitor Progress.** Monitor the aspiration collection canister volume and replace the canister when approaching capacity. Periodically assess thrombus removal progress with contrast venography. Monitor the patient\'s hemodynamic status throughout the procedure.

7\. **Procedure Endpoint.** Continue the thrombectomy procedure until satisfactory thrombus removal is achieved, as determined by venographic assessment, or until a clinical endpoint is reached based on the physician\'s judgment. If satisfactory thrombus removal cannot be achieved during the initial session, extended infusion therapy may be considered (see Section 8.4).

**[8.4 Extended Infusion Therapy (Optional)]{.underline}**

In cases where complete thrombus removal is not achieved during the initial thrombectomy session, the VascuClear Catheter may remain in situ for up to 72 hours for extended thrombolytic infusion therapy. The decision to employ extended infusion therapy is made by the treating physician based on the degree of residual thrombus, the patient\'s clinical status, and the anticipated benefit of continued thrombolytic delivery.

During extended infusion therapy, the following guidelines should be observed:

1\. **Impeller Deactivation.** The impeller should be deactivated during the period of extended infusion. The console drive cable may be disconnected from the catheter during extended infusion if desired.

2\. **Aspiration.** Aspiration may be intermittently activated at the physician\'s discretion to remove any thrombus material that has been lysed during the infusion period. When aspiration is not active, the aspiration lumen should be flushed periodically with heparinized saline to maintain patency.

3\. **Thrombolytic Infusion.** Maintain thrombolytic infusion at a physician-determined rate through the integrated infusion lumen. The infusion rate and total duration of therapy are determined by the treating physician based on clinical judgment, patient response, and institutional protocols.

4\. **Patient Monitoring.** The patient should be monitored in an appropriate care setting during extended infusion therapy. An intensive care unit (ICU) or step-down unit is recommended. Continuous hemodynamic monitoring, including blood pressure, heart rate, and oxygen saturation, should be maintained. Serial laboratory monitoring, including complete blood count, fibrinogen levels, and coagulation parameters, should be performed per institutional protocol.

5\. **Catheter Position Verification.** Catheter position should be checked daily with appropriate imaging (fluoroscopy or radiography) to confirm that the catheter has not migrated from the treatment site.

6\. **Monitoring for Complications.** Monitor for signs of bleeding, catheter migration, access site complications, or infection throughout the period of extended infusion therapy.

7\. **Procedure Following Extended Infusion.** Following completion of extended infusion therapy, a repeat thrombectomy procedure may be performed to remove any residual thrombus, or the catheter may be removed if the treating physician determines that adequate thrombolysis has been achieved.

**[8.5 Catheter Removal and Post-Procedure Care]{.underline}**

1\. Deactivate the impeller by reducing impeller speed to zero and powering off the impeller drive from the console.

2\. Deactivate the aspiration pump.

3\. Disconnect the thrombolytic infusion line from the catheter infusion port.

4\. Disconnect the drive cable connector and aspiration tubing from the catheter.

5\. Under fluoroscopic guidance, remove the VascuClear Catheter over the guidewire. Withdraw the catheter slowly and smoothly. If resistance is encountered, do not force removal; assess catheter position fluoroscopically and take corrective action as needed.

6\. Remove the guidewire from the introducer sheath.

7\. Remove the introducer sheath from the access vein.

8\. Achieve hemostasis at the vascular access site per institutional protocol. Manual compression, compression devices, or other hemostatic measures may be employed as appropriate.

9\. Perform completion venography through a separate venous access, if indicated, to assess the procedural result and evaluate the degree of thrombus removal and restoration of venous flow.

10\. Monitor the patient post-procedure per institutional standards. Post-procedural monitoring should include assessment of access site integrity, vital signs, hemoglobin and hematocrit levels, and symptoms or signs of procedural complications. Adverse events to be monitored include, but are not limited to, access site bleeding, hematoma formation, vessel injury, and hemolysis.

11\. Dispose of the VascuClear Catheter, aspiration tubing set, infusion line connector, and collection canister as biohazard medical waste in accordance with institutional protocols.

**[9. Drug Delivery Information]{.underline}**

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System includes an integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen designed for simultaneous local delivery of thrombolytic agents at the thrombectomy site. This integrated lumen is a core functional feature of the device and enables pharmacomechanical thrombectomy --- the concurrent application of mechanical thrombus maceration and localized thrombolytic drug infusion --- within a single catheter platform.

The thrombolytic agent is not provided with the device and is not a component of the VascuClear 3000 system. The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is a medical device; the thrombolytic agent is a separately regulated pharmaceutical product. The prescribing physician is responsible for selecting the appropriate thrombolytic agent based on clinical judgment, institutional protocols, and the drug manufacturer\'s prescribing information.

Compatible thrombolytic agents include, but are not limited to, tPA (tissue plasminogen activator), such as alteplase, reteplase, and tenecteplase, as well as other physician-specified thrombolytic agents that may be delivered through a catheter-based infusion system. The physician should consult the thrombolytic agent manufacturer\'s prescribing information for approved indications, dosing, reconstitution, and administration instructions.

No specific dosing guidance is provided by Clearfield Medical Devices. Thrombolytic agent dosing should follow the prescribing physician\'s clinical judgment and institutional protocols. Factors influencing dosing include the extent and chronicity of the thrombus, the patient\'s weight, bleeding risk profile, and the planned duration of therapy.

No dilution instructions are provided by Clearfield Medical Devices. The physician should prepare the thrombolytic agent according to the drug manufacturer\'s instructions for reconstitution and dilution. The final concentration and volume of the thrombolytic agent solution should be appropriate for infusion through the catheter infusion lumen at the intended flow rate.

Drug compatibility with catheter lumen materials has not been independently verified by Clearfield Medical Devices. The physician should exercise clinical judgment when selecting agents for delivery through the device. The infusion lumen is constructed of medical-grade polyurethane; however, specific compatibility testing with individual thrombolytic agents, diluents, or drug formulations has not been performed.

The recommended infusion flow rate through the integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen is 2--10 mL/min, as determined by the treating physician. Flow rates outside this range have not been evaluated and may result in inadequate drug delivery or excessive infusion pressure within the lumen.

**[10. Troubleshooting]{.underline}**

The following table provides guidance for common operational issues that may be encountered during use of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System. If a problem persists after performing the corrective actions listed below, discontinue use of the device and contact Clearfield Medical Devices Technical Support.

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Symptom**                                        **Possible Cause**                                   **Corrective Action**
  -------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Impeller fails to activate                         Drive cable not properly connected to catheter hub   Verify that the drive cable connector is fully seated and locked into the catheter proximal hub. Disconnect and reconnect if necessary.

  Impeller fails to activate                         Console not powered on or power cord disconnected    Verify that the console power cord is connected to a functioning electrical outlet and that the console power switch is in the \"ON\" position.

  Impeller fails to activate                         Console self-check error                             Restart the console and allow the self-check to complete. If the error persists, do not use the console; contact Clearfield Medical Devices Technical Support.

  Aspiration flow reduced or absent                  Kink in aspiration tubing                            Inspect the aspiration tubing for kinks, bends, or external compression. Straighten the tubing and re-establish flow.

  Aspiration flow reduced or absent                  Collection canister full                             Check the collection canister volume. Replace the canister if it is approaching or has reached capacity.

  Aspiration flow reduced or absent                  Clot obstruction in aspiration lumen                 Attempt to clear the obstruction by briefly increasing aspiration pressure (do not exceed −650 mmHg). If the obstruction cannot be cleared, consider catheter removal.

  Catheter difficult to advance                      Guidewire malposition or vessel tortuosity           Verify guidewire position under fluoroscopy. Consider repositioning the guidewire or using a stiffer guidewire. Do not force the catheter.

  Catheter difficult to advance                      Introducer sheath too small                          Confirm that the introducer sheath is 11 Fr or greater. Consider upsizing the sheath if catheter passage is restricted.

  Infusion lumen blocked                             Precipitate or clot within infusion lumen            Flush the infusion lumen with heparinized saline. If the obstruction persists and patency cannot be restored, discontinue infusion through the catheter and consider catheter removal.

  Console displays error code                        Various system faults                                Refer to the VascuClear Console Operator Manual for the complete error code reference table. Follow the corrective actions specified for the displayed error code. If the error cannot be resolved, discontinue use and contact Technical Support.

  Unusual vibration felt during impeller operation   Catheter kink or impeller contact with vessel wall   Reduce impeller speed immediately. Inspect the catheter under fluoroscopy for kinking or malposition. Reposition the catheter if necessary. If vibration persists, deactivate the impeller and consider catheter removal.
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[11. Maintenance and Reprocessing (Console Only)]{.underline}**

The VascuClear Console (Model VC3000-D) is a reusable electromechanical device designed for repeated clinical use. The following maintenance and reprocessing instructions apply exclusively to the console. All catheter components (VascuClear Catheter, aspiration tubing set, thrombolytic infusion line connector, and collection canister) are single-use and must be disposed of after each procedure.

**Cleaning.** After each procedure, clean the exterior surfaces of the console with a hospital-grade disinfectant wipe. Pay particular attention to the drive cable connector port, control panel, and LCD display. Do not immerse the console in liquid or allow liquids to enter the console enclosure. Do not autoclave the console.

**Inspection.** After cleaning, inspect the drive cable connector port for debris, dried blood, or other contaminants. Remove any visible debris using a dry, lint-free cloth. Inspect the power cord and drive cable connector for damage.

**Preventive Maintenance.** Annual preventive maintenance is recommended per Clearfield Medical Devices service guidelines. Preventive maintenance includes calibration verification, electrical safety testing, and inspection of internal drive mechanisms. Preventive maintenance should be performed by Clearfield-authorized service personnel only.

**Firmware Updates.** Console firmware updates are to be performed only by Clearfield-authorized service personnel. Do not attempt to modify or update the console firmware without authorization from Clearfield Medical Devices.

**Expected Service Life.** The VascuClear Console has an expected service life of five (5) years or 500 procedures, whichever comes first. At the end of the expected service life, the console should be evaluated by Clearfield-authorized service personnel to determine whether continued use is appropriate.

**[12. Storage and Handling]{.underline}**

**[Catheter Storage]{.underline}**

Store the VascuClear Catheter in its original sealed packaging in a clean, dry area at room temperature. Recommended storage temperature range is 15--30°C (59--86°F). Protect from direct sunlight, excessive moisture, and extreme temperatures. Do not freeze.

Do not use the catheter if the packaging is opened, damaged, or shows evidence of compromise to the sterile barrier. Do not use after the expiration date printed on the package label. The expiration date indicates the last date through which the sterility and functional integrity of the catheter are assured under recommended storage conditions.

**[Console Storage]{.underline}**

Store the VascuClear Console in a clean, dry environment. When not in use, store the console in the protective carrying case provided by Clearfield Medical Devices. During transport, secure the console in its carrying case to prevent impact damage. Avoid exposure to excessive heat, moisture, or vibration during storage and transport.

**[13. Technical Specifications]{.underline}**

**[VascuClear Catheter (Model VC3000-C)]{.underline}**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Parameter**                       **Specification**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Catheter Diameter                   10 Fr

  Working Length                      65 cm

  Guidewire Compatibility             0.035 inch

  Minimum Introducer Sheath Size      11 Fr

  Impeller Type                       Rotating helical

  Infusion Lumen                      Integrated, single lumen

  Infusion Flow Rate                  2--10 mL/min

  Sterilization Method                Ethylene oxide (EtO)

  Single Use                          Yes

  MRI Safety                          MR Conditional (see Section 6)
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[VascuClear Console (Model VC3000-D)]{.underline}**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Parameter**                       **Specification**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------
  Impeller Speed Range                0--12,000 RPM (variable)

  Aspiration Pressure Range           0 to −650 mmHg

  Display                             LCD with real-time speed, pressure, and status indicators

  Power Supply                        100--240 VAC, 50/60 Hz

  Dimensions (approximate)            30 cm × 25 cm × 15 cm

  Weight (approximate)                4.5 kg

  Device Classification               Class II Medical Device

  Regulation                          21 CFR 870.1250

  Product Code                        GXD

  Software                            Embedded firmware

  MRI Safety                          MR Unsafe (do not bring into MRI scanner room)

  Expected Service Life               5 years or 500 procedures
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is classified as a Class II medical device under 21 CFR 870.1250, Product Code GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\"). The device is intended for commercial distribution in the United States subject to 510(k) premarket notification clearance.

**[14. Symbols and Label Explanation]{.underline}**

The following symbols may appear on the labeling and packaging of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System components. Symbols are used in accordance with ISO 15223-1 (Medical devices --- Symbols to be used with information to be supplied by the manufacturer).

**[Section 510(k) Summary]{.underline}**

**[Pursuant to 21 CFR 807.92]{.underline}**

**510(k) Number: K192847**

**Date of Summary: August 14, 2020**

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Submitter:**                      **Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc., San Jose, California**
  ----------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------
  **Device Trade Name:**              ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter System

  **Common/Usual Name:**              Catheter, Thrombectomy

  **Classification Name:**            Catheter, Thrombectomy

  **Product Code:**                   GXD
